data.title: Trump signs order declaring TikTok sale ready and values it at $14 billion
data.description:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) -President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Thursday declaring that his plan to sell Chinese-owned TikTok's U.S. operations to U.S. and global investors will address the national security requirements in a 2024 law.

The new U.S. company will be valued at around $14 billion, Vice President JD Vance said, putting a price tag on the popular short video app far below some analyst estimates.

Trump on Thursday delayed until January 20 enforcement of the law that bans the app unless its Chinese owners sell it amid efforts to extract TikTok's U.S. assets from the global platform, line up American and other investors, and win approval from the Chinese government.

The publication of the executive order shows Trump is making progress on the sale of TikTok's U.S. assets, but numerous details need to be fleshed out, including how the U.S. entity would use TikTok's most important asset, its recommendation algorithm.

"There was some resistance on the Chinese side, but the fundamental thing that we wanted to accomplish is that we wanted to keep TikTok operating, but we also wanted to make sure that we protected Americans' data privacy as required by law," Vance told reporters at an Oval Office briefing.

Trump's order says the algorithm will be retrained and monitored by the U.S. company's security partners, and operation of the algorithm will be under the control of the new joint venture.

Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping had indicated approval of the plans. "I spoke with President Xi," Trump said. "We had a good talk, I told him what we were doing and he said go ahead with it."

The Chinese embassy in Washington did not immediately respond to a request for comment. TikTok did not immediately comment on Trump's action.

Trump has credited TikTok, which has 170 million U.S. users, with helping him win reelection last year. Trump has 15 million followers on his personal TikTok account. The White House also launched an official TikTok account last month.

"This is going to be American-operated all the way," Trump said.

He said that Michael Dell, the founder, chairman and CEO of Dell Technologies; Rupert Murdoch, the chairman emeritus of Fox News owner Fox Corp and newspaper publisher News Corp, and "probably four or five absolutely world-class investors" would be part of the deal.

The White House did not discuss how it came up with the $14 billion valuation. 

TikTok's Chinese parent, ByteDance, currently values itself at more than $330 billion, according to its new employee share buyback plan. TikTok contributes a small percentage of the company's total revenue.

According to Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives, TikTok was estimated to be worth $30 billion to $40 billion without the algorithm as of April 2025.

Alan Rozenshtein, a professor at the University of Minnesota Law School, said the executive order left unanswered questions, including whether ByteDance would still control the algorithm.

"The problem is that the president has certified the deal, but he has not provided a lot of information on the algorithm," he said.

Chinese media on Friday also painted a different picture of the TikTok agreement, suggesting ByteDance would continue to play a major or operational role.

ByteDance will set up a new U.S. company as part of the restructuring of TikTok's U.S. operations, Chinese media outlet LatePost reported, citing sources.

The new company to be set up by ByteDance will be responsible for e-commerce, branding operations and interconnection with international operations, the report said.

The report also said the joint venture, as described by the White House and valued at $14 billion, would be responsible for U.S. digital security, safeguarding content and software as well as related local businesses.

Another Chinese financial magazine, Caixin, also reported, citing people close to the deal, that ByteDance planned to set up a TikTok U.S. entity that will receive some revenue from the new TikTok joint venture.

The White House and ByteDance did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

ORACLE AND OTHERS TO OWN TIKTOK IN THE U.S.

A group of three investors, including Oracle and private-equity firm Silver Lake, will take a roughly 50% stake in TikTok U.S., two sources familiar with the deal said on Thursday.

A group of existing shareholders in ByteDance will hold a roughly 30% stake, one of the sources said. Among ByteDance's current investors are Susquehanna International Group, General Atlantic and KKR.

Given intense investor interest in TikTok, the 50% stake may still shift, the source noted.  

Oracle and Silver Lake did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

CNBC reported earlier, citing sources, that Abu Dhabi-based MGX, Oracle and Silver Lake are poised to be the main investors in TikTok U.S. with a combined 45% ownership.

MGX did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment on the CNBC report.

Republican House of Representatives lawmakers said they wanted to see more details of the deal to ensure it represented a clean break with China. "As the details are finalized, we must ensure this deal protects American users from the influence and surveillance of CCP-aligned groups," said U.S. Representatives Brett Guthrie, Gus Bilirakis and Richard Hudson.

The agreement on TikTok's U.S. operations includes the appointment by ByteDance of one of seven board members for the new entity, with Americans holding the other six seats, a senior White House official said on Saturday.

ByteDance would hold less than 20% in TikTok U.S. to comply with requirements set out in the 2024 law that ordered it shut down by January 2025 if ByteDance did not sell its U.S. assets.

(Reporting by Jeff Mason and David Shepardson in Washington, Dawn Chmielewski in Los Angeles and Deborah Sophia in Bengaluru; additional reporting by Beijing newsroom and Brenda Goh in Shanghai; Editing by Chris Sanders, Matthew Lewis, Leslie Adler and Kate Mayberry)

By Jeff Mason, Dawn Chmielewski and David Shepardson

data.title: Xiamen Jihong Technology Forecasts Up to 67% Rise in Q3 Profit; Shares Jump 10%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Xiamen Jihong Technology (SHE:002803) expects its attributable profit to grow 45% to 67% to between 90.6 million yuan and 104.1 million yuan in the third quarter from 62.3 million yuan in the year-ago period, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The FMCG full packaging services company's basic earnings per share are anticipated to rise to between 0.21 yuan and 0.25 yuan from 0.17 yuan a year earlier.

Deeper brand building and brand resilience led to the growth, among other factors, the filing said.

Shares jumped 10% during late morning trading on Friday.

data.title: South Korea begins new trial of ousted President Yoon over failed martial law
data.description:
SEOUL (Reuters) -South Korea's ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol appeared in court on Friday at the start of a new trial on obstruction charges after weeks of boycotting a separate ongoing trial for masterminding insurrection by leading a failed martial law bid.

Yoon, 64, appeared noticeably slimmer and his hair greyer and cropped short, after more than two months out of public view in prison and having refused to attend trial since early July citing health reasons.

The conservative former president faces fresh charges including obstruction stemming from attempts to block investigators trying to arrest him in January when his powers had been suspended by parliament's impeachment and he barricaded himself inside the presidential compound.

The new charges were brought by a special prosecutor who was appointed in June and whose team has widened a probe of Yoon and several former government and military officials.

Yoon, who was removed from office in April by the Constitutional Court, is also being investigated by another special prosecutor who has indicted his wife for corruption.

Yoon has denied all wrongdoing and said it was within his powers as president to declare martial law in December. He has rejected all investigations against him as politically motivated.

The new indictment carries a prison term of more than three years on conviction. Yoon already faces the death sentence or life in prison if found guilty on insurrection charges.

(Reporting by Jack Kim; Editing by Kate Mayberry)

data.title: DTXS Silk Road Raises HK$38 Million via Share Placement; Shares Jump 7%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- DTXS Silk Road Investment (HKG:0620) said it completed the placement of 66.8 million new shares at HK$0.60 each, raising net proceeds of about HK$38.4 million, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Shares of the firm were up over 7% in Friday late-morning trade.

The shares were placed in tranches between Sept. 1 and 25 to at least six investors, the filing said.

Proceeds will be used mainly for the promotion, development, and sales of the group's property projects in China, with the balance for general working capital.

Following the deal, the company's total issued shares rose to about 801 million from about 734.2 million, with the new investors holding an 8.3% stake.

data.title: Turkish Airlines agrees 225 Boeing aircraft orders, subject to engine talks
data.description:
ISTANBUL (Reuters) -Turkish Airlines said on Friday it decided to purchase 75 aircraft from Boeing, consisting of 50 firm and 25 option orders for B787-9 and B-787-10 models.

In a statement to the Istanbul stock exchange, it said it said it also completed negotiations with Boeing on the purchase of a total 150 aircraft, consisting of 100 firm and 50 option orders for 737-8/10 MAX models.

It said orders for the 737-8/10 MAX aircraft will be placed subject to the successful conclusion of ongoing discussions with engine manufacturer CFM International.

(Reporting by Can Sezer; Editing by Daren Butler)

data.title: US plans to mandate a 1:1 ratio of domestically manufactured to imported chips, WSJ reports
data.description:
(Reuters) -The Trump administration is planning to ask chip companies to manufacture the same number of semiconductors in the U.S. as their customers import from overseas producers, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday.

Companies that do not maintain a 1:1 ratio over time would have to pay a tariff, the report said citing people familiar with the concept.

(Reporting by Ruchika Khanna in Bengaluru; Editing by Jacqueline Wong)

data.title: Giant Biogene's Controlling Shareholder Raises Stake Past 54%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Giant Biogene Holding Co (HKG:2367) said controlling shareholder Juzi Holding acquired 1.15 million shares in the open market on Sept. 24 and Sept. 25 for about HK$62.9 million, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Following the purchases, Juzi Holding's stake rose to about 583.3 million shares, or 54.47% of the company.

data.title: Human Health's Fiscal 2025 Profit Rises 9%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Human Health (HKG:1419) reported HK$26.3 million in attributable profit for the fiscal year ended June 30, up 8.9% from HK$24.2 million a year earlier, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing late Thursday.

Earnings per share rose to HK$0.069 from HK$0.064 in the prior year.

Revenue increased 9.1% year-on-year to HK$644.4 million, figures showed.

The company declared a dividend of HK$0.03 per share, payable Jan. 5, 2026, to shareholders of record on Dec. 18.

Shares of the healthcare service provider fell over 4% in late morning trade Friday.

data.title: JD Cables Secures Order Worth INR98 Million
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- JD Cables (BOM:544524) has bagged an order worth 98 million Indian rupees from Ma Manasa Vyapaar, according to a BSE filing on Friday.

The contract is for manufacturing, testing, supply, and delivery of XLPE AB cable of 400 kms.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing : Trump to Slap New Tariffs on Pharma, Big Trucks
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

No major economic updates or corporate trading updates expected

Opening Call:

European stock futures were higher after Asia stocks broadly fell. U.S. Treasurys were flat and the dollar weakened. Gold and oil rose.

Equities:

European futures were higher as U.S. President Trump announced many new tariffs overnight, including a large one on drugs from pharmaceutical companies that aren't building plants in the U.S. Other tariffs also included ones on large trucks and home goods, with all of them slated to take effect Oct. 1.

"Rising tariffs and persistent trade tensions have reignited inflation fears," said Josh Hirt, senior economist at Vanguard.

"Right now, companies likely have less flexibility to raise prices than they did in the immediate postpandemic period," he added.

Looking ahead, traders will get the latest look at the personal-consumption expenditures price index, the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation.

Economists expect the reading to come in at around 2.9% year-over-year, above the central bank's inflation target. Any upside surprise is likely to disappoint investors, who are betting the Fed will keep easing monetary policy with interest rate cuts.

Forex:

The U.S. dollar weakened as major currencies consolidated against the greenback ahead of the U.S. August PCE inflation report due later today.

"The risk is PCE inflation surprises to the upside," CBA's Carol Kong said in a research report.

This would cause the market to pare back pricing of Fed rate cuts and push up USD, particularly against the backdrop of resilient U.S. economic growth, the economist and currency strategist said.

Bonds:

Treasury yields were flat after rising in the wake of stronger-than-expected U.S. indicators and ahead of August PCE inflation.

The path for Fed cuts is thrown into question after an upward revision in 2Q GDP and other signs of economic resilience.

For now, a 25-basis-point cut is priced for October. U.S. PCE is expected to remain sticky, according to a WSJ survey.

Energy:

Oil rose amid elevated geopolitical tensions. European diplomats have concluded that a recent Russian incursion into Estonia was a deliberate tactic ordered by Russian commanders, ANZ Research analysts said, noting that they have warned that NATO is ready to respond to any further violations of its airspace with full force.

"This ratchets up the tensions in the Russia-Ukraine war and raised the prospect of increased pressure on Russia via additional sanctions on its oil industry," they added.

Metals:

Gold edged higher amid possible position adjustments ahead of major U.S. economic data due later today.

Investors are turning to the August PCE inflation report for confirmation of the Fed's policy outlook, Kudotrade's Konstantinos Chrysikos said in an email.

"Softer data could raise expectations of a dovish stance from the [U.S.] central bank and benefit nonyielding assets like gold," the head of Customer Relationship Management added.

-

Copper held steady, with the three-month copper contract on the LME flat.

Freeport-McMoRan, which operates Indonesia's Grasberg mine, declared force majeure on contracted supplies due to a fatal mudslide accident, which sent prices surging earlier this week.

Sucden Financial analysts expect any additional price increases to stem from speculative interest rather than fundamental factors. They note the Grasberg mine contributes only 3.5% to global mined output, pegging a new support level for copper prices at $10,200 a ton.

-

Iron ore fell, with prices expected to continue to fluctuate, Baocheng Futures analysts said.

The ferrous metal's supply has rebounded, while demand is weakening. The softening demand-supply landscape is adding pressure on overstretched iron-ore prices, they added.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Trump to Slap New Tariffs on Pharma, Big Trucks

WASHINGTON-President Trump announced many new tariffs Thursday, including a large one on drugs from pharmaceutical companies that aren't building plants in the U.S.

"Starting October 1st, 2025, we will be imposing a 100% Tariff on any branded or patented Pharmaceutical Product, unless a Company IS BUILDING their Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Plant in America," Trump posted on his social-media site, Truth Social, without providing details.

Europe's Right-Wing Populists Swerve Left on the Economy

BERLIN-Right-wing populists have a new strategy to woo European voters frustrated with cash-strapped governments: Veer left.

From Sweden to Greece and Austria, parties that for years favored a small state, low taxes, free trade and deregulation are now calling for higher welfare benefits, subsidies and protectionism-often alongside pro-market positions.

Trump Confirms He Will Block Israel From Annexing West Bank

President Trump said he would block Israel from annexing the West Bank, issuing a new ultimatum to head off a move that other Western and Middle Eastern powers warned could further inflame regional tensions and jeopardize Israel's normalized ties with some Arab nations.

"I will not allow Israel to annex the West Bank. I will not allow it. It's not going to happen," Trump told reporters in the Oval Office on Thursday, publicly reiterating a pledge he made to Arab leaders privately earlier this week.

Microsoft Disables Some Cloud Services Used by Israel's Defense Ministry

Microsoft has disabled the Israeli Defense Ministry's access to certain services and subscriptions, after finding evidence that the ministry used the tech company's cloud services to surveil Gaza citizens.

The software company made the move after an internal investigation indicated Israel's Defense Ministry violated Microsoft's terms of service by using its Azure cloud services for surveillance, according to a person familiar with the matter. The company probe is ongoing.

Trump Signs Executive Order to Keep TikTok Alive

Trump signed an executive order on Thursday that is designed to pave the way for a TikTok deal between the U.S. and China.

The executive order is another step in the Trump administration's efforts to keep TikTok running in the U.S. after Congress sought to ban the social media app in 2024. The deal can't be considered complete until China signs off on it, industry experts said. Trump said on Thursday that he got the go-ahead from Chinese President Xi Jinping.

Intel Seeks Help From Apple and Others as U.S. Backs Comeback Bid

Intel Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan has been hustling to secure investments and customer commitments needed for the chipmaker's comeback.

Among the companies Intel has approached about investments or manufacturing partnerships are Apple and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, according to people familiar with the matter.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Friday

06:00/SWE: Aug Foreign trade

06:00/DEN: Aug Retail Sales Index

07:00/SVK: Aug PPI

07:00/SPN: 2Q Final GDP

08:00/AUT: Sep Austria Manufacturing PMI

08:00/ITA: Sep Consumer Confidence Survey

08:00/ITA: Sep Business Confidence Survey

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-26-25 0015ET

data.title: Generali Advances on Natixis Deal: Extended Timeline and No Penalties
data.description:
(Alliance News) – With the MPS takeover bid on Mediobanca now behind it, which shifted 13.1% of Leone’s capital to Siena, Generali is refocusing on its asset management negotiations with Natixis, according to Il Corriere della Sera on Friday.

During Tuesday’s board meeting, CEO Philippe Donnet provided an update on talks with BPCE, introducing three new elements to the framework agreement: no penalties in the event of withdrawal, an extended deadline until the end of the year to finalize the deal, and binding approval from both boards before triggering golden power, the newspaper reports.

The deal, considered strategic amid ongoing sector consolidation, could give rise to a group managing EUR 1.9 trillion in assets, making it a global leader in the insurance segment.

Il Corriere notes that Donnet reiterated the agreement will not be pursued “at any cost” and that discussions with the government—which has so far remained lukewarm on the matter—will be necessary.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Zhongtai Securities Issues 2 Billion Yuan Bonds
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Zhongtai Securities (SHA:600918) issued 2 billion yuan worth of 169-day bonds, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The bonds carry a coupon of 1.74%, the filing said.

Shares rose 1% during late morning trading on Friday.

data.title: Future Bright Mining Seeks HK$28 Million via Offering
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Future Bright Mining (HKG:2212) said it is looking to raise HK$28 million via a share offering, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the marble trader gained over 34% in late morning trade Friday.

The company is seeking investors for nearly 210.7 million shares, priced at HK$0.133 each, to raise funds primarily for working capital.

The shares represent 20% of the company's existing issued share capital and 16.7% on an enlarged basis.

VBG Capital is acting as the placing agent for the offering.

data.title: Ericsson Trades Ex-Dividend Today on Nasdaq Stockholm at SEK 1.42 Per Share
data.description: Telecommunications supplier Ericsson is trading ex-dividend today, September 26, on Nasdaq Stockholm, without the right to the upcoming dividend of SEK 1.42 per share. The annual dividend amounts to SEK 2.85 per share, corresponding to a direct yield of 3.7 percent based on the latest closing price for the B share at SEK 77.38 per share.

data.title: Thailand to expand cooperation with China on semiconductors, battery production, PM says
data.description:
Bangkok, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Thailand will expand cooperation with China on semiconductors and battery production, the country's Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul said on Friday.

He was speaking at the Thailand China Cooperation Expo.
(Reporting by Chayut Setboonsarng; Editing by David Stanway)

data.title: Indian pharma stocks fall after Trump's new drug tariffs
data.description:
(Reuters) -Indian pharmaceutical stocks fell 2.6% on Friday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a 100% tariff on branded and patented drugs, effective October 1.

All 20 constituents of the index were lower as of 9:30 a.m. IST. Heavyweight Sun Pharmaceutical Industries fell 3.4%.

The U.S. takes slightly more than a third of India's drug exports, mostly cheaper generics, which rose 20% to about $10.5 billion in fiscal 2025.

The near term impact of the tariffs is likely to be limited, as India mainly exports generics, ICICI Securities research analyst Pankaj Pandey said in a note.

"That being said, uncertainty still remains whether complex generics and biosimilars will come under tariff embargo in the future," Pandey added.

(Reporting by Ananta Agarwal in Bengaluru; Editing by Nivedita Bhattacharjee)

data.title: Hero MotoCorp Appoints Chief Human Resources Officer
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Two-wheeler manufacturer Hero MotoCorp (NSE:HEROMOTOCO, BOM:500182) has appointed Madhuri Mehta as chief human resources officer with effect from Oct. 1, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Mehta will be joining the company from Emaar India, where she served as chief human resources officer and head of communities.

data.title: Anchorstone Proposes 1-for-10 Share Consolidation to Meet Hong Kong Listing Requirement
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Anchorstone (HKG:1592) said its board proposed a 1-for-10 consolidation of its shares, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the marble and granite supplier fell over 6% in late morning trade Friday.

The move, if approved, will reduce the company's existing issued shares to 241,296,281 consolidated shares from 2,412,962,811.

The firm said it is undertaking a consolidation in view of prevailing trading prices to meet a Hong Kong listing requirement that shares trade at a minimum threshold of HK$0.01.

The company will call a meeting of shareholders to deliberate on the proposal.

data.title: Unicredit could make board more German to win over Commerzbank - FT
data.description:
Sept 26 (Reuters) - Commerzbank AG:

  • Unicredit could make board more German to win over Commerzbank - FT

data.title: India's Waaree Energies plunges on US probe into suspected solar tariff evasion
data.description:
(Reuters) -Shares of India's Waaree Energies fell 4.5% to 3,288.9 rupees on Friday after U.S. customs officials said they were investigating if the solar panels maker sidestepped tariffs on Chinese-made cells and panels by labeling them as made in India.

The U.S. Customs and Border Protection said in a memo on Friday that there was reasonable suspicion that Waaree had failed to label some of its imported products as subject to antidumping and countervailing duties Washington has imposed for years.

The agency said it was taking steps to protect U.S. revenues, such as requiring cash deposits from the renewable energy firm while the investigation is ongoing.

Waaree did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

Shares of the company have risen 37% since their trading debut in October 2024.

(Reporting by Aleef Jahan in Bengaluru, Editing by Harikrishnan Nair)

data.title: BrightGene Chairman Proposes Up to 20 Million Yuan Share Buyback; Shares Slide 10%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- BrightGene Bio-Medical Technology (SHA:688166) Chairman Yuan Jiandong proposed that the company repurchase its shares for between 10 million yuan and 20 million yuan, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The shares should be bought back within 12 months, the filing said.

Shares plunged 10% during late morning trading on Friday.

data.title: Hitachi Announces NVIDIA AI Factory to Accelerate Physical AI Innovation
data.description:
Hitachi Ltd. announced the establishment of a global Hitachi AI Factory based on NVIDIA AI Factory reference architecture, yielding a centralized infrastructure designed to accelerate the development and deployment of physical AI solutions across Hitachi?s core business sectors. The AI Factory is powered by Hitachi iQ with NVIDIA HGX B200 systems featuring NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs; Hitachi iQ M Series with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Server Edition GPUs; and NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet networking platform. This strategic initiative directly supports Hitachi's ambition to create and deploy AI that interacts with the real world.

The AI Factory will also enable Hitachi to further expand HMAX?the company?s family of AI-enabled solutions solving a wide-range of complex problems in the Mobility, Energy, Industrial and Technology sectors. The centralized AI Factory provides Hitachi?s global teams with a powerful unified AI computing infrastructure to run applications and AI workflows developed on the NVIDIA full-stack AI platform, which includes NVIDIA AI Enterprise for production-grade AI as well as NVIDIA Omniverse libraries for simulation, industrial scale, and physically accurate digital twins. This infrastructure allows for the rapid development and deployment of advanced physical AI models.

These models will acquire and interpret information from physical environments via cameras and sensors; determine the next steps to take; and execute actions based on that data. The strategic collaboration between Hitachi and NVIDIA is becoming a key engine for solving complex real-world problems, accelerating social innovation.

data.title: Hong Kong corporate earnings week ahead
data.description: Sept 26 (Reuters) - Please be advised that there are no corporate earnings scheduled for Hong Kong (.HSI) in the week ahead. (Compiled by Bengaluru Newsroom)

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on September 26
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Friday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

ECONOMY

ISTAT releases September consumer and business confidence data (0800 GMT).

DEBT

Treasury sells 3.5-4.0 billion euros of new 2.85% BTP bonds due February 1, 2031; 1.5-2.0 billion euros of 3.60% BTP bonds due October 1, 2035; 1.0-1.5 billion euros of CCTeu bonds due April 15, 2043; 1.0-1.25 billion euros of 4.00% BTP Green bonds due April 30, 2035. Subscriptions close at 0900 GMT.

COMPANIES

BRUNELLO CUCINELLI

The luxury group on Thursday rejected reports by short sellers that it is still conducting substantial business in Russia, saying its reduced activities in the area are in full compliance with European Union regulations.

BANCA MEDIOLANUM

Fitch Ratings said on Thursday it had upgraded the bank's long-term default rating (IDR) to 'BBB+' from 'BBB', following the upgrade of Italy's sovereign rating.

CREDITO EMILIANO

Fitch Ratings said on Thursday it had upgraded the lender's IDR to 'BBB+' from 'BBB', following the upgrade of Italy's sovereign rating.

TELECOM ITALIA (TIM)

The phone group said on Thursday it had picked former Bank of Italy official Alessandra Perrazzelli to fill a seat on the its board of directors.

JUVENTUS

Board meeting on FY results.

DIARY

Milan, ECB board member Piero Cipollone attends ECB-CEPR-Bocconi university conference on payments (0715 GMT).

Olbia, the EXPO of countries bordering the Mediterranean-MEDFEST continues; ends on September 28. On Friday expected attendees include Energy Services Manager (GSE) President Paolo Arrigoni, Environment and Energy security Minister Gilberto Pichetto Fratin, Infrastructure and Transport Minister Matteo Salvini or Deputy Minister Edoardo Rixi, Louis Vuitton CEO Pietro Beccari.

Pescara, "Abruzzo Economy Summit, States-General of the Economy" conference ends; on Friday expected attendees include Poste Italiane Direttore Generale Giuseppe Lasco, Monte dei Paschi di Siena Chairman Nicola Maione, via videoconference Labour Minister Marina Calderone and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani.

Venice, Industry Minister Adolfo Urso attends official opening ceremony of the exhibition "Identitalia - The Iconic Italian Brands" (1400 GMT).

data.title: CSPC Pharma Wins China Nod for Lecanemab Biosimilar Trials
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- CSPC Pharmaceutical (HKG:1093) said its Lecanemab injection has received approval from China's National Medical Products Administration to begin clinical trials, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The product is a biosimilar to LEQEMBI and is intended for the treatment of mild cognitive impairment due to Alzheimer's disease and mild dementia, the filing said.

data.title: Db insurance to acquire U.S.-based insurer Fortegra
data.description:
Sept 25 (Reuters) - Tiptree Inc:

  • DB Insurance to acquire U.S.-based insurer Fortegra
  • Tiptree Inc - deal valued at about $1.65 billion in cash

data.title: US investor Stonepeak aims to raise $4 billion for second Asia infrastructure fund, source says
data.description:
SINGAPORE/HONG KONG (Reuters) -U.S.-based investor Stonepeak is seeking to raise as much as $4 billion for its second Asia-focused infrastructure fund, a source with direct knowledge of the plan said, as investors seeking lucrative long-term returns drive a wave of new investments in the region.

Stonepeak expects to reach $1 billion, or a quarter of its target, by the end of this month, four months after its launch in May, according to the source, who declined to be named as the details are not public.

The fundraising comes just a year after the firm closed its first Asia-focused fund at $3.3 billion, signalling swift capital deployment and strong investor interest in the asset class.

A spokesperson for Stonepeak, which began investing in Asia in 2019 and manages $76.3 billion in assets globally, declined to comment on the fundraising.

The firm's fundraising plan comes as many countries in Asia, faced with geopolitical uncertainties and climate change targets, seek to increase their infrastructure spending to sustain economic growth and living standards.

Developing Asia needs to spend around $1.7 trillion a year on infrastructure between 2023 and 2030 "if the region is to maintain its growth momentum, eradicate poverty, and respond to climate change," according to the Asian Development Bank.

In Asia, besides the traditional infrastructure areas, global investors said they were also increasingly targeting sectors that are opening up new investment opportunities, such as data centres and logistics.

Cold storage logistics, for example, has become a key focus for Stonepeak, driven by changing diets and rising protein demand, said Hajir Naghdy, the firm's senior managing director and head of Asia and the Middle East.

A "multi-decade" trend of rapid urbanisation has created a generational infrastructure investment opportunity in Asia, he added.

Other global investors have also been increasing their firepower in Asia. KKR has begun fundraising for its third pan-Asia infrastructure fund, though it did not disclose the target size, filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission showed.

KKR's new vehicle is expected to exceed the $6.4 billion raised for its Asia infrastructure fund last year, said a separate source with knowledge of the fundraising plan.

KKR declined to comment.

If successful, the fundraisings by Stonepeak and KKR would add more than $10 billion in deployable capital to the region.

Available capital for the Asia infrastructure sector stood at $30 billion at the end of last year, according to data from Preqin.

STABLE ASSETS

KKR is applying private equity-style strategies to businesses that are slightly higher on the risk spectrum but still behave like infrastructure firms, said Andrew Jennings, a managing director at the U.S.-based firm.

In July, KKR, which has about 30 infrastructure-focused investment staffers in Asia, struck a deal to buy ProTen, one of Australia's largest broiler chicken growers, from pension fund Aware Super.

"It is really trying to find assets that are stable and steady, that will give you yield and some capital growth," Jennings said.

I Squared Capital, which entered Asia in 2012, is also planning to add around 10 more people in the region over the next two to three years and will open a Seoul office in the coming months, senior partner Harsh Agrawal said.

The U.S.-based infrastructure investor is betting on the energy transition, digital infrastructure and logistics as long-term growth drivers.

In June, Switzerland-based Energy Infrastructure Partners launched its first Asia office in Singapore. It will seek to raise and invest capital into energy transition assets and originate deals in markets like Australia, Japan and Korea.

"We see Singapore as our launch pad to build relationships, deploy capital, and eventually originate investments in some of the region's most advanced markets," said the firm's partner and co-head of investments Peter Schümers.

(Reporting by Yantoultra Ngui and Kane Wu; Editing by Sumeet Chatterjee and Thomas Derpinghaus)

By Yantoultra Ngui and Kane Wu

data.title: Khoon Group's Loss Widens in Fiscal 2025; Shares Fall 10%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Khoon Group (HKG:0924) reported a loss attributable to owners of SG$2.1 million for the year ended June 30, widening from a loss of SG$8,896 a year earlier, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Shares of the firm were down 10% in Friday morning trade.

Loss per share came in at SG$0.0021, compared with breakeven in the prior-year period.

Revenue rose to SG$77.7 million from SG$69.5 million.

data.title: Microsoft halts services to Israeli military unit amid probe into surveillance of Palestinians
data.description:
(Reuters) -Microsoft said on Thursday it has disabled some services used by an Israeli military unit after preliminary evidence supported a media investigation that reported mass surveillance of Palestinian phone calls.

A joint investigation published in August by the Guardian and other media outlets found that an Israeli military agency was making use of Microsoft's Azure software to store countless recordings of mobile phone calls made by Palestinians living in the Israeli-occupied West Bank and Gaza.

That prompted an internal review by Microsoft.

"We do not provide technology to facilitate mass surveillance of civilians," Microsoft President Brad Smith said in a company blog.

The review is ongoing, but details of the Israel Ministry of Defense's (IMOD) consumption of Azure storage capacity in the Netherlands and the use of AI services supported the Guardian's reporting, he said.

The decision to "cease and disable" specific IMOD subscriptions, including the use of specific cloud storage and AI services, would not impact Microsoft's cybersecurity services to Israel and other countries in the Middle East, he added.

A spokesperson for the Israeli ministry told NBC News it had no comment on Thursday's announcement.

At the time the media investigation was published, Israel's military told the Guardian its work with firms like Microsoft was based on "legally supervised agreements." The military later added that Microsoft "is not and has not been working with the (Israeli military) on the storage or processing of data."

Pro-Palestinian groups such as the Council on American Islamic Relations (CAIR) and a tech industry worker-led campaign group named No Azure for Apartheid welcomed the decision.

"This is a welcome step and a point of vindication for those brave tech workers who stood up and protested," Imraan Siddiqi, the executive director of CAIR's Washington state chapter, said. The groups have demanded that Microsoft cut all ties with the Israeli government.

Microsoft has been among the most prominent of companies that have faced protests over ties to Israel as the humanitarian crisis in Gaza from Israel's military assault has mounted and images of starving Palestinians, including children, have sparked global outrage.

Some recent protests on company premises have led to firings of some employees who took part, including two who joined a sit-in at Smith's office. Microsoft says the terminations followed breaches of company policies and the on-site demonstrations had created what it called significant safety concerns.

The Guardian's investigation was conducted with Israeli-Palestinian publication +972 Magazine and Hebrew-language outlet Local Call.

Israel's two-year long assault on Gaza has killed tens of thousands of people and internally displaced Gaza's entire population. Multiple rights experts, scholars and a U.N. inquiry say it amounts to genocide.

Israel calls its actions self-defense after the October 2023 attack led by Palestinian Hamas militants that killed 1,200 people and in which more than 250 were taken hostage.

(Reporting by Juby Babu in Mexico City and Kanishka Singh in Washington; Editing by Tasim Zahid and Edwina Gibbs)

By Juby Babu and Kanishka Singh

data.title: Kwan Yong Sees Sharp Rise in Fiscal 2025 Profit
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Kwan Yong (HKG:9998) reported SG$12.8 million in attributable profit for the fiscal year ended June 30, rising from SG$1.6 million a year earlier, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Earnings per share was SG$0.016 compared with SG$0.002 in the prior year.

Revenue rose 79% year-on-year to SG$239.9 million, figures showed.

The company also declared a dividend of HK$0.02 per share, payable Jan. 21, 2026, to shareholders of record on Dec. 30, 2025.

Shares of the investment firm gained over 20% in late morning trade Friday.

data.title: Frontier Services Group to Sell 3 Cessna Jets for $3 Million
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Frontier Services Group (HKG:0500) agreed to sell three Cessna Citation Bravo 550 light jets to Protea Group Management Services for $3 million, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the company fell over 2% in morning trade Friday.

The sale will help the company raise funds for the technical upgrade and maintenance of other aircraft.

data.title: Frasers and Neave to Dispose of 50% Stake in Malaysian Joint Venture
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Frasers and Neave (SGX:F99) decided to dispose of a 50% stake in joint venture company in Malaysia, Vacaron Company, to Tan and Tan Developments, according to a Friday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The move is part of the company's strategy to focus on its manufacturing, distribution, marketing and sale of its beverage, dairy and food products.

The joint venture with Frasers Property Malaysia owns a parcel of land measuring 5.149 hectares.

data.title: China Smarter Energy's H1 Loss Narrows
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China Smarter Energy Group (HKG:1004) reported HK$98.9 million in attributable loss for the first half of 2025, narrowing from a loss of HK$409.7 million a year earlier, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Loss per share was HK$0.0106 compared with HK$0.0437 in the prior year.

Revenue for the period rose 6.5% year-on-year to HK$38.2 million, figures showed.

Shares of the electric company remain suspended from trading.

data.title: Thailand budgets $1.46 bln for co-payment scheme to start in Oct, minister says
data.description:
Bangkok, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Thailand is preparing a budget of 47 billion baht ($1.46 billion) for a co-payment scheme that will start in October, Paradorn Prissananantakul, minister at the Prime Minister's office, said on Friday.

($1 = 32.21 baht)

Reporting by Chayut Setboonsarng, Panarat Thepgumpanat; Editing by David Stanway

data.title: Heineken Launches EUR2 Billion Three-tranche Notes Issue
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Dutch brewing company Heineken (HEIA.AS) issued three-tranche notes worth 2 billion euros, set to list on the Luxembourg Stock Exchange.

The first 500 million-euro tranche offers a coupon of 2.565% and will mature on Oct. 3, 2028. The other two tranches, worth 750 million euros each, carry coupons of 3.505% and 3.872%, respectively, with maturities on May 3, 2034, and Oct. 3, 2037, according to a Thursday release.

BNP Paribas, Deutsche Bank, ING, JP Morgan, and Rabobank were the active bookrunners for the issue.

data.title: Breton Technology Wins Potential Contract For Electric Mining Trucks; Shares Jump 19%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Breton Technology (HKG:1333) won a potential contract for its autonomous electric mining trucks from Xinjiang Mingyang Mining Construction Group, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the electric-powered engineering machinery company gained nearly 19% in late-morning trade Friday.

The firm entered into a so-called strategic cooperation agreement where both sides agreed to cooperate on electrified and autonomous mining transportation.

As part of that agreement, Xinjiang Mingyang Mining intends to acquire at least 1,000 electric mining trucks from the company by Sept. 22, 2028.

data.title: India central bank likely steps into NDF market to support rupee after US tariff jolt
data.description:
Mumbai, Sept 26 (Reuters) - The Reserve Bank of India likely stepped into the non-deliverable forwards (NDF) market before the local market opened on Friday to support the rupee, five traders told Reuters, as the currency came under pressure after the US announced steep tariffs on imported branded drugs.

The Indian rupee was on course to open near its all-time low of 88.7975 against the US dollar but recovered following the intervention. It opened at 88.6850 at 9:00 a.m. IST (0330 GMT), nearly flat from its close in the pervious session.

The rupee has fallen about 0.5% in September amid worries over the impact of steep US tariffs on Indian exports and higher H1-B visa fees.

(Reporting by Jaspreet Kalra and Dharamraj Dhutia; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)

data.title: CSC Unit to Acquire 51% Stake in AccX via Share Subscription; CSC Shares Rise 17%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- CSC Holdings (HKG:0235, SGX:C06) said its wholly owned unit agreed to subscribe for 255,000 convertible preference shares in AccX Limited for about $4.37 million, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Hong Kong-listed shares of the engineering services provider surged over 17% in Friday morning trade.

The investment will give CSC an indirect 51% stake in AccX, which will become a non-wholly owned subsidiary of the group upon completion.

Proceeds from the investment will be used to repay start-up loans, fund working capital and R&D, and support expansion, CSC said.

The company added that the acquisition aligns with its strategy to diversify into Southeast Asia's financial services market and may create synergies with its earlier purchase of Citystate Savings Bank in the Philippines.

AccX and its affiliates provide digital payment services in Southeast Asia, the filing said.

data.title: Volkswagen to Temporarily Halt Production at Two German EV Plants
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Volkswagen (VOW.F) plans to temporarily halt production at two German factories that exclusively produce electric vehicles amid slower-than-expected demand for EVs.

Citing a spokesperson for the automaker's Zwickau site, Bloomberg News reported Thursday that production will be halted for one week starting Oct. 6. Meanwhile, the Emden plant has trimmed employee hours and is expected to shut down production for several days, the report added, citing people familiar with the situation.

Volkswagen did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Materials Sector Rises, Healthcare Falls
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Materials stocks advanced almost 1% at midday Friday.

Stronger-than-expected second-quarter US GDP data decreased expectations for rate cuts in the US. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indices fell 0.5% and the Dow Jones index declined almost 0.4%.

Fortescue (ASX:FMG) finalized the acquisition of Spanish renewable technology firm Nabrawind, consolidating its previous minority stake to take full ownership. Under the firm's agreement with XCMG, it is expected to supply up to half of Fortescue's future fleet of 300 to 400 zero-emissions 240-tonne haul trucks, with phased deliveries planned from 2028 to 2030.

Liebherr, with manufacturing facilities in the US, is expected to provide at a minimum the other half of the haul truck fleet and electric excavators under a partnership with Fortescue.

On the flip side, the healthcare sector fell over 1% after sector heavyweight CSL (ASX:CSL) declined over 2% in recent trading.

The decline came after US President Donald Trump warned of a 100% tariff on imports of branded or patented pharmaceutical products, effective Oct.1, unless the company is constructing a manufacturing plant in the US.
